mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP signals broad economic expansion, raising corporate earnings and investor confidence, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 rises but the average 20-day forward SPX return is negative or not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below the 6% threshold signals a tightening labor market and stronger consumer spending, which supports corporate profits and lifts equity prices over the following quarter.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE falls below 6%, the average 60-day forward SPX return is negative or the signal provides less than 55% hit rate in a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When both the federal funds rate and inflation are low, monetary policy is highly accommodative, compressing discount rates and encouraging equity investment, driving positive forward returns.
failure_condition: If the interaction term (FEDFUNDS * CPIAUCSL) in a regression on 20-day forward returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) or has the wrong sign.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high unemployment (above 6%), positive nonfarm payroll surprises reflect genuine labor market healing, strengthening consumption and equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If when UNRATE > 6%, an increase in PAYEMS is followed by negative average 20-day SPX returns or a hit rate below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in industrial production (month-over-month growth exceeding 0.5%) signals solid economic activity and resilient supply chains, pushing equity prices higher within the next week.
failure_condition: If after INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5% the 5-day forward SPX return is negative in more than 60% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistently rising retail sales indicate robust consumer demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and supports a bullish outlook for equities over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward SPX return is not positively correlated with RSAFS level changes (rank IC < 0.05) over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate above the 0.25% threshold initiates a tightening cycle that raises the discount rate and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: If after FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.25%, the average 20-day forward SPX return is positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and declines in unemployment jointly signal a self-reinforcing recovery in construction and labor markets, boosting broad equity market returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increasing and UNRATE decreasing over 3 months
failure_condition: If the joint condition occurs but the average 60-day forward SPX return is negative or the signal produces less than 55% accuracy over any 3-year window.
